# UC-13.1.59 · Prometheus Query Engine Active Concurrency Limit Timeouts

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Availability &middot; **Status:** Draft

*We watch the metric stack and its limits so we notice scrape, storage, and query pressure before charts lie or go empty.*

---

## Description

Concurrency saturation blocks Grafana and automation queries during incidents.

## Value

Reveals when horizontal query sharding or limits need tuning.

## Implementation

Do not log raw query parameters containing secrets; scrub at ingest.

## SPL

```spl
index=prometheus sourcetype="prometheus:log"
| search "query" AND ("timeout" OR "concurrency" OR "max concurrent")
| stats count by host, user
| where count>=20
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (timeouts by user), Line chart (rate).

## Known False Positives

Noisy periods after retention, target, or scrape changes. We compare with the Grafana or Prometheus view for the same minutes.

## References

- [Prometheus documentation — monitoring Prometheus](https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/)
- [Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector](https://docs.splunk.com/observability/en/gdi/opentelemetry/opentelemetry.html)
